title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Trump Makes New Pick To Replace Tulsi Gabbard
stories.summary:
Trump Makes New Pick To Replace Tulsi Gabbard. President Donald Trump on Thursday nominated former Securities and Exchange Commission Chair Jay Clayton to be the next Director of National Intelligence, replacing outgoing intelligence chief Tulsi Gabbard.

Clayton currently serves as U.S. attorney for the Southern District of New York, having previously led the SEC from May 2017 through December 2020 during Trump’s first ... Al Jazeera: Trump to pick US attorney Jay Clayton for director of national intelligence.

Al Jazeera reports that nomination comes amid pushback over Donald Trump's selection of Bill Pulte as acting director of the agency.

links.excerpt: Nomination comes amid pushback over Donald Trump's selection of Bill Pulte as acting director of the agency.

stories.title: Bill Gates the Victim: Claims Epstein Used His Personal Infidelities as Blackmail
stories.summary:
Bill Gates the Victim: Claims Epstein Used His Personal Infidelities as Blackmail. Billionaire Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates claimed Wednesday disgraced sex offender Jeffrey Epstein attempted to use information about his personal life — including his multiple marriage infidelities — to blackmail him, copies of his first closed-door testimony to the House Oversight Committee detail.

The post Bill Gates the Victim: Claim. BBC: What we learned from Bill Gates' deposition on Epstein.

BBC reports that the billionaire testified to Congress about his relationship with the late convicted sex offender.

stories.title: Top secrets come spilling out
stories.summary:
Top secrets come spilling out.

In 1917, British analysts deciphered a coded message the German foreign minister sent to one of his country's diplomats vowing to begin "unrestricted submarine warfare" and seeking to win over Mexico with a promise to "reconquer the lost territory in Texas, New Mexico and Arizona" if the US entered the world war.

When it became public, the Zimmerman Telegram caused a sensation, helping propel the US into the conflict against Germany.

When it became public, the Zimmerman Telegram caused a sensation, helping propel the US into the conflict against Germany.

stories.title: Trump vows to ‘take back’ DC if socialist mayoral candidate wins
stories.summary:
Trump vows to ‘take back’ DC if socialist mayoral candidate wins. President Donald Trump on Thursday vowed to exert federal control over Washington, D.C., again if Councilwoman Janeese Lewis George wins the primary election next week and the general election in November.

Lewis George, a self-described socialist, is one of many candidates running in the Democratic primary on June 16.

A reporter compared Lewis George to […]. Washington Times: Trump: Feds will take over D.C. if Democratic socialist wins mayoral election.

Washington Times reports that president Trump said Thursday the federal government will take over Washington if Democratic socialist Janeese Lewis George is elected mayor.

stories.title: Kennedy Center board to appeal order striking Trump’s name from building
stories.summary:
Kennedy Center board to appeal order striking Trump’s name from building. The Kennedy Center‘s board of trustees voted on Thursday to appeal an Obama-appointed federal judge’s order that requires President Donald Trump‘s name to be removed from the performing arts building by Friday.

The board vote came hours before all references to Trump in the Kennedy Center’s signage, digital materials, and branding were supposed to be […]. Washington Times: Kennedy Center board seeks delay of deadline to remove Trump's name.

Washington Times reports that president Trump's handpicked board at the Kennedy Center is mounting a last-minute effort to keep his name on the facade of the iconic performing arts facility before a court-ordered deadline to remove it by Friday.

stories.title: Appeals court allows Trump to collect 10% global tariffs for now
stories.summary:
Appeals court allows Trump to collect 10% global tariffs for now. A federal appeals court is allowing President Donald Trump to collect his temporary 10% global tariffs for now while the legal challenges against the trade levies proceed through the judicial system.

In a Thursday ruling, the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit in Washington, D.C., concluded the Trump administration‘s case in defense of […]. Washington Times: Appeals court says U.S. government can keep collecting 10% tariffs for now.

Washington Times reports that the U.S. government can continue collecting the 10% worldwide tariff it imposed in February while legal challenges to the levies continue to work their way through the courts, a federal court ruled Thursday.

stories.title: NT government says Claravale land-clearing dispute is now settled
stories.summary:
NT government says Claravale land-clearing dispute is now settled. For years, the Northern Territory government has been locked in a legal battle with Claravale Station over allegations its owners cleared hundreds of hectares without a permit.

But it is not the first time the government has announced the settlement deal.

stories.title: How to Love the World by Ilka Tampke review – a woman is trapped by a fallen tree
stories.summary:
How to Love the World by Ilka Tampke review – a woman is trapped by a fallen tree. The stuck narrator records the minutiae of the forest and her harrowing life in a purposeful novel that demands a slow read but doesn’t always reward it A large branch falls to the forest floor one morning.

Moments later, a woman named Nellika returns to consciousness.

“Belly-down, cheek jammed against dirt, trunks horizontal, the track’s edge a disorienting vertical. She had opened her eyes to the world on its side.” The branch struck her across the back, and now she is trapped, in gre.

stories.title: Pool’s new app turns your screenshots into something useful
stories.summary:
Pool has launched a new application designed to transform the way users manage their digital screenshots. The app automatically organizes captured images into personalized collections, eliminating the need for manual sorting.

Beyond simple storage, Pool tracks down the original web links associated with saved content. This feature allows users to easily rediscover products, recipes, travel ideas, and other items they intended to revisit but had previously lost track of.

links.headline: Pool’s new app turns your screenshots into something useful
links.excerpt: Pool's new app automatically sorts screenshots into personalized collections, tracks down the original links behind saved content, and helps you rediscover products, recipes, travel ideas, and other things you meant to revisit.

stories.title: AI absolutism is breaking our brains. The apocalyptic future we’re being sold isn’t inevitable
stories.summary:
AI absolutism is breaking our brains. The apocalyptic future we’re being sold isn’t inevitable.

Nor is the dreamy promise that this tech will unlock boundless potential and productivity Everything we hear about artificial intelligence is conflicting, and hearing about it feels inescapable. AI is terrible.

AI is wonderful. It will break the world.

It will transform the future. It’s essential to embrace it.

It’s a moral imperative to abstain from using it. Already, AI is projected to generate nearly unfathomable amounts of revenue.

In the last quarter of 2025, it represented n.

stories.summary:
Ateneo probes athletes’ deaths as more agencies tackle case. MANILA, Philippines — Ateneo de Manila University on Thursday announced an independent fact-finding investigation into the deaths of student-athletes Rene Clert Baterbonia and Chukwuemeka Divine Adili, as multiple government agencies intensified separate inquiries into the drowning incident that claimed the lives of the two basketball players during an off-campus activity in Aurora province.

stories.title: MSU-GenSan seeks gov’t help to rise from quake
stories.summary:
Mindanao State University-General Santos City has requested immediate assistance from the national government following significant damage caused by a recent earthquake. The university estimates that infrastructure repairs will cost approximately 900 million pesos.

Chancellor Shidik Zed Abantas noted that current engineering assessments are preliminary and likely to increase as structural evaluations continue across campus facilities.

links.headline: MSU-GenSan seeks gov’t help to rise from quake
links.excerpt: GENERAL SANTOS CITY, Philippines — Mindanao State University-General Santos City (MSU-GSC) campus has appealed for immediate national government assistance after the powerful earthquake damaged infrastructure worth an estimated P900 million. stories.title: Education leaders urge cross-sector action to reverse PH learning crisis
stories.summary:
Education leaders from the Philippines and other nations convened in Manila to address the country's persistent learning crisis, emphasizing that reversing it requires a more integrated approach. They argued for stronger coordination across sectors to connect education with broader child development services.

The discussions highlighted the need for sustained commitment and cross-sector collaboration to tackle ongoing educational challenges effectively.

stories.title: Senators deliver messages of hope, unity, patriotism on Independence Day
stories.summary:
As the Philippines marks its 128th Independence Day, Senate President Pro Tempore Sherwin Gatchalian and other senators emphasized that national freedom must translate into improved lives for citizens through stronger institutions and responsive governance.

President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. issued a separate message urging Filipinos to protect truth from distortion as the country faces modern challenges distinct from those of past revolutionary heroes.

links.headline: Marcos to Filipinos on Independence Day: Protect truth from distortion

stories.summary:
Manila Water boosts network resilience vs combined ‘habagat’ and El Niño. MANILA, Philippines — Manila Water has activated its full range of operational preparedness measures as the southwest monsoon or “habagat” sets in, amid evolving El Niño conditions that are expected to influence rainfall patterns in the coming months.

stories.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
stories.summary:
Australia has become the first nation to implement a ban on social media for children, with the regulation taking effect in late 2025. The policy is designed to mitigate various risks associated with online platforms, including cyberbullying, addiction, and exposure to predators.

This legislative move places Australia at the forefront of global efforts to regulate digital spaces for minors. Other countries are now considering similar measures as they evaluate the impact of social media on youth development and safety.

links.headline: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
links.excerpt: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

stories.title: I was addicted to my phone – but one screen time hack actually made a difference
stories.summary:
A recent personal account highlights a surprisingly effective method for reducing smartphone addiction, as detailed in a new article from The Guardian. The writer utilized Apple's Screen Time app to track usage and discovered they were spending approximately eight hours per week browsing platforms like Reddit.

This data-driven approach allowed the author to identify specific habits contributing to excessive screen time. By implementing a targeted hack based on these insights, the individual reported a meaningful reduction in daily phone use.

links.headline: I was addicted to my phone – but one screen time hack actually made a difference

stories.summary:

stories.title: AI wealth boom sending San Francisco home prices surging: ‘It’s ridiculous’
stories.summary:
A surge in artificial intelligence wealth is driving San Francisco home prices to unprecedented levels, according to recent reports. Employees at leading AI firms are realizing substantial financial gains following a wave of initial public offerings.

This influx of capital has intensified competition in the Bay Area’s already expensive housing market. The rapid appreciation of property values has drawn attention from observers who describe the current conditions as extreme.
